prompts: Gamma rays and X-rays are both part of the electromagnetic spectrum and are distinguished primarily by their origin and energy levels. Gamma rays originate from nuclear decay and have higher energy levels compared to X-rays, which are emitted by electronic processes involving changes in atomic electron shells. In terms of wavelength, gamma rays have shorter wavelengths than X-rays, indicating higher photon energy. Both are used for diagnostic and therapeutic purposes in medicine, but due to their higher energy, gamma rays are more penetrating and hazardous than X-rays.
